title: Add Alm project structure and transaction form
bodyText:
This PR adds the functionality to search for a tx hash. For now, it only displays the status of the transaction.
It validates the status of the transaction introduced with the following states:

Correct with one bridge message
Correct with a lot of bridge messages (in this case it will show a list of messages to select one)
Correct but without bridge messages
Failed transaction
Does not exists/Pending transaction (in this case it will retry to load the tx receipt in case it is confirmed later)

The project supports different theme styles. For now, the styles are very simple and we can improve it later after having the core functionality working.
There are 3 URL paths:

/ the base path to introduce to search for the tx hash
/chainId/txHash by entering this link it will automatically start the search for that tx hash in the specified chain. In the case of multiple messages, it will display the option to select one.
/chainId/txHash/messageIndex same as the other path but it will also automatically select the specified message of the transaction

There is a deployed Demo here for Sokol-Kovan AMB bridge: https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app/
Some examples :

Not found tx https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app/77/0x115921f63c63f176d20ebad1e4650fcd0fbeb9f2923d9c5e8671d534544e35e9

title: Add old events processing in AMB monitor
Add ALM transaction verification detection
bodyText:
This PR adds the following features:

Detect that validators are waiting for block confirmations for the request
Detect that the responsible validator is waiting for block confirmations to execute the signatures on the foreign network
Detect the submitted verifications by the validators by calling messagesSigned and affirmationsSigned methods
Detect the message execution finalization by listening to the events RelayedMessage and AffirmationCompleted

Another change is that In the transaction information page now the form is not displayed. I removed it to save some space on the page and also it looks cleaner to me. You can go back to the form by clicking the title. If it is not so obvious, we can also add some button to go back.
You will notice some differences in the displayed information with the design from the slides:

The Validator table does not include the Age column, this is because we don't have the transaction to display the information about it. In the future, we can add the column if we provide the functionality to discover this information.
The Executed by table is also displayed for foreign to home transactions. Despite the execution not being done in a separate transaction, it helps to display which validator executed the message (by being the last to submit the signature) and to display the time since the execution including the link to the transaction. If you think displaying this is confusing, we can think of better alternatives.

The demo page was updated with these changes: https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app
Edit: The demo also includes latest changes from #357
Example Home to Foreign transaction
https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app/77/0x5325279181e04916f4a3e95fc0b34c02afc0d6392e79b53fe58b2ff69f8f2779
Example Foreign to Home transaction
https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app/42/0x480c5f8abe241e52f756d9d5ea9faeb8b633d44a6441cdfc0cf194b3489e5699
The following features will be addressed in futures PRs:

Detect Failed transactions
Detect Pending transactions

title: Add ALM failed validator transactions detection
bodyText:
Built on top of #356
This PR adds the following features:

Detects failed transactions from validator when trying to verify the messages
Detects failed transactions from validator when trying to execute signatures in the foreign network

For the failed transactions for verification in home network it supports the blockscout API. It allows us to filter the transactions by timestamp. So it filters from the moment of the transaction to the next day to reduce the amount of data requested. We can review this parameter and/or approach looking for a better one.
https://github.com/poanetwork/tokenbridge/blob/6185969b09350aaa0aa90e191cb61eece428491a/alm/src/utils/getConfirmationsForTx.ts#L64-L65
For the failed transactions for executing signatures in the foreign network, it supports the Blockscout API and the Etherscan API. Here it also filters using timestamps. In this case, it looks for the next 3 days after the CollectedSignatures event timestamp. We can review this parameter and/or approach looking for a better one.
https://github.com/poanetwork/tokenbridge/blob/3cb4a655a07b58bf6d4f0bafc7af690d3a0d0c30/alm/src/utils/getFinalizationEvent.ts#L56-L57
In the case of using Etherscan, it only allows to filter by blocks and not by timestamp. So in that case, it first calls the Get Block Number by Timestamp endpoint to get the block numbers.
The changes are included in the demo: https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app/
Detecting pending transactions will be added in a separate PR.

title: Add ALM new styles
bodyText:
Built on top of #371
This PR adds the following changes:

New UI styles. Closes #368
Remember the last used chain. Closes #372
Add a new definition of the ABIs used inside the project sources. 3aa951c

I noticed that the bundle size of the app after running the build command was kind of big. After inspecting it, I detected that the whole commons package with all the ABIs and defined method was being included in the bundle. I'm not sure why webpack (the tool used by create-react-app to generate the build) is failing at removing the resources that are not used for that module, finding the cause will require some more in-depth analysis. To avoid this issue, I included the ABI used by ALM as part of the project.
Before sizes
531.01 KB          build/static/js/main.3b16c90d.chunk.js
438.47 KB          build/static/js/2.35b98736.chunk.js
762 B              build/static/js/runtime~main.a8a9905a.js

After sizes
419.25 KB (-19.22 KB)  build/static/js/2.dab69f3e.chunk.js
12.08 KB (-518.92 KB)  build/static/js/main.c6b2322e.chunk.js
762 B                  build/static/js/runtime~main.a8a9905a.js

title: ALM docker improvements
bodyText:
This PR introduces the following changes:

Fixes an error with web3 dependencies that caused the app to failed to compile using docker
Allow specifying etherscan key. Example: ALM_FOREIGN_EXPLORER_API=https://kovan.etherscan.io/api?apikey=YourApiKeyToken
Updated docker-compose file to bind ports. The new variable PORT is used for this.
Updated Dockerfile to serve the production build of the app. This also allows reducing the size of the docker image to ~950MB

Example of building and running with docker:
docker-compose build

And then
docker-compose up

or
docker run -ti -p 8080:8080 -e PORT=8080 --rm alm_alm:latest

title: Migrate to GitHub actions
bodyText:
This PR migrates the CI configuration from CircleCI to GitHub Actions. Closes #411

All jobs were successfully migrated
An example of the successful run: https://github.com/k1rill-fedoseev/tokenbridge-ci/actions/runs/234414305
The following secrets are required for this configuration: DOCKER_USERNAME and DOCKER_PASSWORD. The CI runner will use docker hub for caching required e2e images.

As a part of the CI migration, the following optimisations were made:

Metamask extension used in ui-e2e tests, was repackaged to the crx3 format, that is only supported in the newest browsers. Now ui-e2e tests can be run on the latest Google Chrome version, which comes preinstalled in hosted runners.
Docker images base was bumped from node:8 to the node:10.
Built and caching process was drastically improved by building only the images those parts were changed.
Ultimate tests were improved by utilising the already built oracle image instead of building a fresh one.

The following minor bugs were found and fixed:

Default file mode in ansible copy command was changed from 0666 to 0600. So, I have explicitly specified the 0666 mode in these cases as it was suggested by the corresponding warning.
monitor-e2e tests for erc-to-native mode were fixed by adding a missing if statement.
alm unit tests were fixed, in accordance with the changes from 8cf73d5
timeouts in monitor-e2e were increased, since in some cases they were to small
oracle unit tests for gas price service were fixed after the changes applied in f252ed2

title: Possibility to allow/block specific addresses in erc-to-native mode
bodyText:
This PR allows validator to create a special files, allowance_list.txt and block_list.txt, which will filter out some of the CollectedSignatures requests.

If allowance_list.txt is not empty, then only requests with either sender or recipient being in the list will be processed.
If block_list.txt is not empty, then only requests with neither sender nor recipient being in this list will be processed.
In all other cases, every request is processed as it is.

bodyText: It was found that Infura providers return null for pending transactions instead of the tx object with "blockNumber": null. This PR updates the behaviour for stucked transactions and makes the checking logic more universal.

title: Small improvements of the execution waiting status description in ALM
bodyText:
Due to gas price spikes the transaction with signatures could stuck in the pool for some time. Currently, the description of the execution waiting status in ALM did not cover this situation before.
The state description is also updated on https://docs.tokenbridge.net/about-tokenbridge/components/amb-live-monitoring-application/alm-transition-states#home-greater-than-foreign-state-transitions.

title: Centralized handling all collected signatures requests
bodyText: Currently the oracle that sends the finalising signature is responsible for transferring all the signatures to the Foreign chain to finalize the bridge requests originating in the Home chain. Sometimes (e.g. few oracles has no enough funds for the account in the Foreign chain), it is useful to allow one oracle to handle all CollectedSignatures events.

title: Update requirements for higher bound of gas price
bodyText: Analysis of the transactions made in the Ethereum Mainnet in August and September of 2020 shows that the gas price could be much higher than the current higher bound -- 250 gwei. Due to this limit the oracles transactions were minted with significant delays.

title: Remove access lists tests from ultimate test suites
Handle JSONRPC error codes in web3 providers
bodyText:
The Infura provider reports error codes as an response for a JSON RPC request. E.g.
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2020 09:48:24 GMT
< Content-Type: application/json
< Content-Length: 88
< Connection: keep-alive
< Vary: Origin
< 
* Connection #0 to host mainnet.infura.io left intact
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32603,"message":"request failed or timed out"}}

This PR is to let the oracles to to rely on the returned error code to switch to a fallback RPC URL.

title: Add monitor script for detecting failed AMB messages
Fix resend of stuck pending transactions
bodyText:
This PR is to address behavior when due to congestion in the chain, the privileged oracles' transactions with 0 gasprice could getting stuck. Later, when the oracle is trying to resend them it will get error message that the transaction with the same hash is already in the txpool, and it will not be added back to the resend queue for further check if it was eventually mined.
As the result another transaction of the same oracle with the same nonce will re-write the original transaction and the bridge message will not be transferred at all.

Always search transactions by block ranges, don't use starttimestamp/endtimestamp options
Add a hook for manual searching of the closest block number given some transaction timestamp
Fetch account transactions incrementally in small block range batches, caching the obtained transaction list

title: Fix ALM react subscriptions leading to duplicated RPC requests
bodyText:
I have found a couple of problems related to the usage of useEffect ReactJS hooks in the ALM app. This PR should address them, the number of made requests decreased in my local tests.
I also believe that previously used subscribe/unsubscribe pattern does work properly. If unsubscribe() is called while the Promise in useEffect is still in the pending state, once finished, it will create a new setTimeout subscription, while it should not do so. This can generate unnecessary requests as well. So, I have fixed the way useEffect is used by using isCancelled variable.

title: Add blocks range limit for logs polling
bodyText:
Addresses the issue found under #555
Polling events from the unbounded block range can be a source of several problems:

Some RPC endpoints have a strict limit for the maximum range limit for eth_getLogs (e.g. standard BSC JSON-RPCs)
If requested block range contained lots of events, oracle won't be able to handle all of them in the fixed amount of time (defined by ORACLE_MAX_PROCESSING_TIME). It will fail to make any progress from such point.

This PR fixes both problems by introducing a configurable range size limit for both sides.

This PR adds a web3 provider wrapper that intercepts and modifies eth_getLogs requests into a batch of two simultaneous requests (eth_getLogs + eth_blockNumber). title: Allow to store validator keystore file in the docker swarm secret
fix/bsc rate limit
Refetch old logs ranges to see if there are missed events
bodyText:
The idea was to introduce a way of refetching old block ranges, without generating a lot of useless RPC requests. Here is how it can be achieved:

Oracle records all seen events in the Redis sorted set, where set score for each event is its block number, and the value is its unique identifier (e.g. txHash + logIndex)
Once per Y blocks, oracle re-requests logs for (HEAD - X - Y)..(HEAD - X) period instead of following regular logs processing iteration. For each event, it checks if its identifier is present in the Redis sorted set. Then it reprocesses all events which were not present in Redis.
Oracle removes checked range of events from the Redis sorted set, so that its size will not grow indefinitely.

This way, usual circumstances will require only a single RPC request per Y blocks to be made, which is very efficient.

Bumps json5 from 0.5.1 to 1.0.2.

Release notes
Sourced from json5's releases.

v1.0.2

Fix: Properties with the name __proto__ are added to objects and arrays. (#199) This also fixes a prototype pollution vulnerability reported by Jonathan Gregson! (#295). This has been backported to v1. (#298)

v1.0.1
This release includes a bug fix and minor change.


Fix: parse throws on unclosed objects and arrays.


New: package.json5 has been removed until an easier way to keep it in sync
with package.json is found.


v1.0.0
This release includes major internal changes and public API enhancements.


Major JSON5 officially supports Node.js v4 and later. Support for Node.js
v0.10 and v0.12 have been dropped.


New: Unicode property names and Unicode escapes in property names are
supported. (#1)


New: stringify outputs trailing commas in objects and arrays when a space
option is provided. (#66)


New: JSON5 allows line and paragraph separator characters (U+2028 and U+2029)
in strings in order to be compatible with JSON. However, ES5 does not allow
these characters in strings, so JSON5 gives a warning when they are parsed and
escapes them when they are stringified. (#70)


New: stringify accepts an options object as its second argument. The
supported options are replacer, space, and a new quote option that
specifies the quote character used in strings. (#71)


New: The CLI supports STDIN and STDOUT and adds --out-file, --space, and
--validate options. See json5 --help for more information. (#72, #84,
and #108)


New: In addition to the white space characters space \t, \v, \f, \n,
\r, and \xA0, the additional white space characters \u2028, \u2029,
and all other characters in the Space Separator Unicode category are allowed.


New: In addition to the character escapes \', \", \\, \b, \f, \n,
\r, and \t, the additional character escapes \v and \0, hexadecimal
escapes like \x0F, and unnecessary escapes like \a are allowed in string
values and string property names.


New: stringify outputs strings with single quotes by default but
intelligently uses double quotes if there are more single quotes than double
quotes inside the string. (i.e. stringify('Stay here.') outputs
'Stay here.' while stringify('Let\'s go.') outputs "Let's go.")
